subject: Re: Demande de tarif Agent de Voyage – Séjour professionnel à Saint-Martin (5–10 mars)
body:
Chère Marion,
J’espère que vous allez bien.
Merci encore pour vos précédentes réponses et pour votre aimable invitation à venir découvrir Le Martin lors de mon passage à Saint-Martin.
Étant donné que le mois de mars est complet, je souhaitais simplement savoir si vous auriez éventuellement des disponibilités pour un court séjour professionnel entre le 11 et le 15 avril.
Même une ou deux nuits me permettraient déjà de découvrir l’hôtel dans le cadre de mes repérages pour ma clientèle.
Dans tous les cas, si cela devait également être complet, je serais bien sûr très heureux de passer vous rencontrer et visiter la propriété lors d’un prochain passage sur l’île.
Au plaisir de vous lire et, je l’espère, de découvrir prochainement Le Martin.
Très cordialement,
Michael Dias
Conseiller en voyages

subject: Re: Booking 22/23 Jan
body:
Good morning. Thank you for your detailed response and I totally understand and agree that you were doing what you thought was best for us. There is a saying “no good deed goes unpunished”.
But I’m the customer and so it’s like me ordering and paying for a black car and going to the garage to pick it up and the owner saying I got you a red one as it’s so much nicer.
I also really appreciate your offer but we won’t be coming back - not because it isn’t a fabulous hotel (it is really beautiful & you should be proud) but because I didn’t have a very happy stay there.
So, because I ordered a black car and got given a red one, I will approach Booking.com customer services for a refund.  I’m glad that we tried to resolve it between ourselves first.
Kind regards
Jane
Sent from my iPhone

subject: Booking 22/23 Jan
body:
Good morning
I’m writing to you with (unfortunately) a complaint. I wanted to let you know and see if we can resolve this in person rather than me going through Booking.com etc.
I’m someone who has worked a lot internationally. I love travelling.
I also have a passion for design and architecture. When I book places to stay I sometimes use air BnBs, sometimes hotels, sometimes cheap, sometimes expensive. I do a lot of research. I use Conde Nast, Small Hotels of the World, Mr & Mrs Smith, I-escape, Special Places to Stay and keep recommendations and magazine clippings. I’m very specific about what I want. I’m happy to spend money on the right place.
In this case I wanted a stylish hotel with adjoining rooms. That is what I spent hours looking for and what I booked on Booking.com. One Family

subject: Re: FORA Travel - Le Martin Boutique Request - May 2026
body:
Thank you! This is super helpful.
Can you confirm:
- you have a pool on site?
- is there a beach that is walkable to the property?
- there a restaurant and bar on site?
- is there a spa?
- what is the cancellation policy?
The women will be celebrating a few 35th birthdays
Thank you!
Ashley

subject: Booking
body:
Hi Marion,
I’m planning a trip to St. Martin with my husband for my birthday and we’re looking at either March 2–6 or March 3–7 just the two of us.
We’d love to know if you have any special rates or promotions for those dates? The website has been hard to navigate and the prices are only showing in English with no way for me to switch to USD, so I’m having a bit of trouble figuring out the best rate.
Would you be able to share:
• Any special offers / promo codes available?
• Whether booking direct has extra perks?
• Approximate USD pricing for your best deal in early March?
Thanks so much — looking forward to celebrating on the island! 🌴✨
Warmly,
Siobhan Valentine
Get
Outlook for iOS
